sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Public Library
The library re-opened last month

sections.instruction: Choose the correct answers and complete the table as instructed.

questions.content: Which TWO age groups are taking increasing numbers of holidays with BC Travel?
Which TWO are the main reasons given for the popularity of activity holidays?
options: B clients learn a useful skill
C clients learn about a different culture
D clients are excited by the risk involved
E clients find them good value for money

questions.content: How does BC Travel plan to expand the painting holidays?
options: A by adding to the number of locations
B by increasing the range of levels

questions.content: Why are BC Travel’s cooking holidays unusual?
options: A they only use organic foods
B they have an international focus
C they mainly involve vegetarian dishes

questions.content: What does the speaker say about the photography holidays?
options: B the tutors are also trained guides
C advice is given on selling photographs

sections.instruction: Complete the flow chart below. Choose SIX answers from the options A-H given below. Then choose the correct letter A, B or C for Questions 27-30.
sections.passage_or_context: STAGES IN DOING A TOURISM CASE STUDY

questions.content: Natalie and Dave agree one reason why so few people visit Horton Castle is that
options: B it is difficult to get to
C there is little there of interest

questions.content: Natalie and Dave agree that the greatest problem with a visitor centre could be
options: B finding a big enough space for it

questions.content: What does Dave say about conditions in the town of Horton?
options: A there is a lot of unemployment
B there are few people of working age
C there are opportunities for skilled worker

questions.content: According to Natalie, one way to prevent damage to the castle site would be to
options: A insist visitors have a guide
B make visitors keep to the paths

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context: The effects of environmental change on birds

questions.content:
Mercury (Hg)
• Highly toxic
• Released into the atmosphere from coal
• In water it may be consumed by fish
• It has also recently been found to affect birds which feed on (31) {{BLANK}}
Research on effects of mercury on birds
• Claire Varian Ramos is investigating:
o The effects on birds’ (32) {{BLANK}} or mental processes e.g. memory
o The effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird’s (33) {{BLANK}})
• Findings:
o Songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less (34) {{BLANK}}
o This may have a negative effect on birds’ (35) {{BLANK}}
• Lab based studies:
o Allow more (36) {{BLANK}} for the experimenter
Implications for humans
• Migrating birds such as (37) {{BLANK}} containing mercury may be eaten by humans
• Mercury also causes problems in learning (38) {{BLANK}}
• Mercury in a mother’s body from (39) {{BLANK}} may affect the unborn child
• New regulations for mercury emissions will affect everyone’s energy (40) {{BLANK}}
